t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客！不许在休息时间打扰帕姆！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you shouldn't disturb me during my break time!

t_ch: 跟我聊会儿不行嘛？
t_en: Can't you chat with me for a while?

t_ch: 现在不行，等会儿可以。现在是帕姆的听歌放松时间。
t_en: Maybe later. Now is the time for me to listen to songs and unwind.

t_ch: 回来一趟，你怎么不理我…
t_en: Why are you ignoring me when I'm finally back...

t_ch: 没有不理你，帕姆只是想自己独处一会儿，之后再理你！
t_en: I just want some me-time before spending time with you!

t_ch: 非常抱歉，但我下次还敢！
t_en: I'm sorry, and I'll do it again next time!

t_ch: 呜哇！你怎么回事帕！
t_en: Whoa! What's wrong with you?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客！我再强调一遍——不许欺负列车长！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let me repeat this again — do not bully Pom-Pom!

t_ch: 就算是列车长，也需要一点私人空间的。
t_en: Even the conductor needs a little privacy.

t_ch: 唔，这次就算了，下次可不能这么调皮了，记住了帕？
t_en: I'll forgive you this time. Can you promise me you won't pull this off again?

t_ch: 停停停！你看看你干的好事！
t_en: Stop, stop, stop! Look at what you've done!

t_ch: 一地的脚印！刚刚打扫完的地板就被你毁啦！
t_en: Footprints are everywhere! I've just cleaned the floor!

t_ch: 那你再扫一遍？
t_en: Can you clean it again?

t_ch: 那之前的清洁工作不就白费了嘛！
t_en: Wouldn't it be a waste of my previous effort?

t_ch: 抱歉，我不是故意的。
t_en: Sorry, I didn't mean to dirty the floor.

t_ch: 呜，好帕，列车长也不是小心眼的人。
t_en: Uhm, fine. I'm not petty anyway.

t_ch: 哪有脚印，我怎么没发现！
t_en: Footprints? I see nothing here!

t_ch: 有！帕姆说有就是有。
t_en: There they are! When I say there are footprints, there are.

t_ch: 不管你怎么说，总之你把地板弄脏了。
t_en: Anyway, you've dirtied the floor.

t_ch: 在你来之前，我清洁完的地板都是光可鉴人的…但现在……
t_en: Before you came, the floor was all shiny as a mirror after I cleaned it... But now...

t_ch: 哎，你自己去看看就知道啦！
t_en: Tsk. Just take a look and see for yourself!

t_ch: 地板光可鉴人，你压根看不到一丝「脚印」的痕迹。
t_en: Shiny as a mirror indeed, without a single footprint.

t_ch: 地板上干干净净，在列车灯光的照耀下，就连空气中的浮尘也看不见。
t_en: The floor is spotless. Under the illumination of the lights, you cannot spot any speckles of dust in the air.

t_ch: 你反复观察，不断尝试，在侧身<unbreak>45</unbreak>度注视下仔细搜寻，终于发现——
t_en: You glue your eyes on the floor, trying to find the footprint. Under a <unbreak>45</unbreak>-degree sideways glance, you finally notice...

t_ch: 一条约有拇指长度的划痕，但这显然不是所谓的「脚印」。
t_en: There's a scratch about the length of your thumb — it is obviously not some "footprint."

t_ch: 怎么样，看到自己的「杰作」了吗？
t_en: Have you seen the "masterpiece" you made?

t_ch: （告知你的见闻）
t_en: (Tell Pom-Pom your observation)

t_ch: …划痕？哎，抱歉，看来是列车长的问题……
t_en: ...A scratch? Ah, I'm sorry. I think I saw it wrongly...

t_ch: 虽说知道是地板的划痕，而不是脚印，那我就不用再扫一遍。
t_en: Since it isn't a footprint, I don't have to mop the floor again.

t_ch: 能省下不少事，按理来说我该高兴才是，但不知为什么总觉得很在意。
t_en: It spares me a lot of trouble. I should be happy, but for some reason, I'm still quite bothered by it.

t_ch: 可能是你有洁癖？
t_en: Do you have mysophobia?

t_ch: 我只是比较爱干净帕？
t_en: I think I'm just a bit of a clean freak.

t_ch: 该不会是…强迫症！
t_en: You might have obsessive-compulsive disorder!

t_ch: 听起来好像蛮严重的，我应该…没有帕？
t_en: That sounds pretty serious to me. I guess... I'm alright?

t_ch: 你太累了，该休息会儿了。
t_en: You're too tired. It's time to take a rest.

t_ch: 帕姆不累，帕姆还要继续整理列车上的事务呢。
t_en: I'm fine. There are things on the Express that I need to handle.

t_ch: 看来车厢的地板需要保养了，可能是我眼花帕。
t_en: Seems like the coach's floor is in need of some maintenance. Maybe my eyesight is failing me.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，误会你了真的很抱歉。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I'm really sorry for misunderstanding you.

t_ch: 帕姆，帕姆会好好反省自己的。
t_en: I—I'll reflect on my mistake.

t_ch: 你回来了？{NICKNAME}乘客！
t_en: Nice to see you again, Passenger {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我听其他乘客说，你在空间站里做了不少好事，像你这么热心肠的乘客可不多啦！
t_en: I've learned about the gracious deeds you did at the space station from the other passengers. It's hard to come across a passenger as kind as you!

t_ch: 怎么样，跟空间站里的人相处得还好吧？
t_en: Did you get along well with the people at the space station?

t_ch: 还行，挺开心的。
t_en: I had quite a good time there.

t_ch: 那就好，之前星穹列车就停靠在空间站数次，跟他们打交道还挺舒服的。
t_en: I'm glad to hear that. The Astral Express has stopped at the space station a couple of times before. They were a pleasant bunch.

t_ch: 就这样吧，不好也不坏。
t_en: There's nothing to write home about.

t_ch: 听上去有点敷衍…倒也可以理解，毕竟…那种地方肯定没法跟列车上比。
t_en: You sound indifferent, which is understandable... After all, the place is nothing like the Express.

t_ch: 感觉有点融不进去…
t_en: I had a hard time blending in...

t_ch: 听上去你不太满意嘛？不过也情有可原，空间站现在好像有不少事情要处理。
t_en: Sounds like you didn't have the time of your life there. It's not hard to imagine why, as the space station has been quite busy lately.

t_ch: 所以{NICKNAME}乘客有在哪儿结交到什么朋友吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, did you make any friends there?

t_ch: 要是有，你可以邀请他们上列车逛逛。
t_en: If you did, you may invite them for a ride on the Express.

t_ch: 咱们这里什么都有，而且，这可不是随便什么人都能上来的！这是很宝贵的邀请！
t_en: We have everything here, and not everyone gets the opportunity to board the Express! That would be a precious invitation!

t_ch: 你有想过要邀请谁上来玩吗？
t_en: Have anyone on your mind you want to extend an invitation to?

t_ch: 黑塔…吧？
t_en: Herta, I guess?

t_ch: 听你这口气，她真的是你的「朋友」吗？
t_en: There isn't much enthusiasm in your voice. Is she really your friend?

t_ch: 不过你也别灰心，可以试试，星穹列车绝对会让每位乘客都宾至如归！
t_en: Don't worry, and feel free to give it a go! Every passenger will feel at home here!

t_ch: 艾丝妲！
t_en: Asta!

t_ch: 呜哇，是那个…空间站站长对吧？
t_en: Wow, it's the Lead Researcher of the space station, right?

t_ch: 帕姆记得她是个挺机灵的小姑娘，帕姆很欢迎她上来做客哦！
t_en: I remember she's quite a witty girl. I'd love to have her as my guest!

t_ch: 阿兰！
t_en: Arlan!

t_ch: 呜哇，是那个…个子小小的家伙对吧？
t_en: Wow, it's the... tiny guy, right?

t_ch: 总是沉默寡言，成天提着一把很重的剑的那个！帕姆听说他是听话又本分的乖孩子，帕姆很欢迎他来做客！
t_en: That quiet guy who's always carrying a bulky sword! I heard he's a nice and responsible kid. I'd love to have him as my guest!

t_ch: 算了，都不熟。
t_en: Forget it. I'm not close with anyone.

t_ch: 怎么可能，别想欺骗帕姆。虽然列车长没跟你一起冒险过，但在其他乘客那里，我也听说了。你明明就跟大家相处得很好嘛！
t_en: How's that possible? I know you must be lying. Although I've never gone on an adventure with you, I heard a lot about you from other passengers. You're getting along well with everyone!

t_ch: 不要自卑，也不要过度谦虚！
t_en: Don't feel inferior about yourself or be too humble!

t_ch: 那就这么说定了帕！有空的话你可以跟他们说一声，让他们来车上逛逛。
t_en: It's a deal! Please invite them over if you have time.

t_ch: 空间站里有月台的帕，列车停靠起来非常方便！
t_en: I can stop the Express at the railway platform of the space station! It's quite convenient.

t_ch: 现在的灯光真的太沉闷了吗…得找个人问问才行帕！
t_en: The lights around here seem a little dull... I should consult someone on this!

t_ch: 诶{NICKNAME}乘客来得正好！请公平公正的回答帕姆的问题吧：你觉得现在这节车厢的灯光，真的太过沉闷了吗？
t_en: Hey, {NICKNAME}! You're here at just the right time! Please offer your unbiased answer to my question: Do you think the lights in this coach are too dull?

t_ch: 沉闷至极！
t_en: Extremely dull!

t_ch: …呜呜，{NICKNAME}乘客说话太直接了！不过也算是发自内心的真实答案帕。
t_en: *sob* That's very direct of you, {NICKNAME}! Still, I appreciate your honesty.

t_ch: 我挺喜欢。
t_en: I like them.

t_ch: 谢谢{NICKNAME}乘客的喜欢！帕姆也觉得还不错，嘿嘿。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}! I think so, too! Hee-hee.

t_ch: 没啥感觉。
t_en: There's nothing special about them.

t_ch: …呜呜，{NICKNAME}乘客说话太残酷了！沉闷的极致就是让人内心毫无波澜！
t_en: *sob* That's very harsh of you, {NICKNAME}! Only extreme dullness can make you think that way!

t_ch: 三月乘客一直都在说车厢的车灯太过单调沉闷了，作为列车长也是很困扰的！
t_en: March's been complaining that the lights in the coaches are too simple and boring. As the conductor, I'm very bothered by it!

t_ch: 我偷偷制定了几套新的车厢灯光方案，但是一直没能找到合适的人选帮帕姆看看效果。
t_en: I've secretly formulated some lighting plans, but I haven't found the right person who can evaluate the lighting effects.

t_ch: 既然今天{NICKNAME}乘客在，那就麻烦你啦！等帕姆去调整一下车厢灯光的调度就好哦。
t_en: Since you're here, {NICKNAME}, I'll count on you to help me! Please wait for me to adjust the lights.

t_ch: 调度已经准备就位，这几种配色都是帕姆精心挑选的，{NICKNAME}乘客只需要给出公平公正的感受就好！
t_en: Everything's ready. These colorways are carefully selected by me. Please tell me what you think about them, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 试试这个「舞动」配色！
t_en: Try the "Disco" colorway!

t_ch: 哼哼，这可是帕姆参考了很多视频之后配出来的得意之作！睁大眼睛，好好感受吧！
t_en: Hehe, it's a masterpiece I created after lots of study and practice! Don't blink, and get ready to be blown away!

t_ch: 绚烂的灯光以及频繁的闪烁突如其来，整个列车仿佛变成了舞厅。
t_en: Various rays of flickering light in vibrant colors take over the Express and turn it into a nightclub.

t_ch: 这…这就是舞动的感觉吧！好…好头晕。
t_en: I... I reckon this is what a disco looks like! My... my head is spinning.

t_ch: 试试这个「忧郁」配色！
t_en: Try the "Melancholic" colorway!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，忧郁的配色可是很难界定的，帕姆读了很多首诗歌才想到这个思路的哦！
t_en: Hehe, it's hard to define the color of melancholy. I only had the inspiration after reading countless poems!

t_ch: 无尽的黑暗突如其来，周遭一切都堕入了黑暗……
t_en: The surroundings suddenly plunge into total darkness...

t_ch: 这…黑夜就是忧郁的最好配色！怎么样，帕姆的感受还…还不错吧？
t_en: Well... Nighttime represents melancholy the best! It... gives off a nice vibe, doesn't it?

t_ch: 试试这个「自然」配色！
t_en: Try the "Natural" colorway!

t_ch: 亲近自然就是最好的放松思路，在这样的列车灯光中一定感觉很舒服吧？
t_en: Embracing nature is the best way to relax your mind. It must be very soothing to be under this kind of lighting.

t_ch: 刹那之间列车车厢变为纯粹的绿色，绿得浓郁，绿得张狂。
t_en: In a flash, the inside of the coach is painted in a pure and intense shade of green.

t_ch: 怎么样？是不是感觉很…很自然，对吧？
t_en: What do you think? It makes you feel very close to nature, doesn't it?

t_ch: 今天就试到这里吧…
t_en: Let's call it a day...

t_ch: 帕…帕姆也觉得有点眼花了。今天就到这里吧……
t_en: M—My vision is getting blurry. I guess that's all for today...

t_ch: 刚才一下子看了太多颜色，帕姆有点头晕…{NICKNAME}乘客也先休息一下，再来告诉帕姆你的选择吧！
t_en: I'm feeling a bit dizzy after looking at too many colors... Please take a break and tell me your choice in all honesty, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，你如果决定好了就告诉帕姆你的答案吧！请公平公正地选出你最喜欢的那种配色！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, tell me your choice when you're ready! Please choose your favorite colorway in all honesty!

t_ch: 我喜欢晕晕五彩斑斓配色。
t_en: I like the dizzying and vibrant colorway.

t_ch: 什么叫「晕晕五彩斑斓」配色啦！唔姆…不过确实那个颜色太闪烁了，帕姆现在都还有点头晕呢。
t_en: That's not the name of the colorway! Well... the colorway is indeed too flashy, and I'm still a bit giddy now.

t_ch: 我喜欢关灯节能纯黑配色。
t_en: I like the energy-saving black.

t_ch: 什么叫「关灯节能纯黑」配色啦！唔姆…不过确实好像有点太黑了，要是有乘客不小心摔跤可就不好了。
t_en: That's not the name of the colorway! Well, it'll be too dark, and passengers might trip over something by accident.

t_ch: 我喜欢绿色通道纯粹配色。
t_en: I like the green-lane colorway.

t_ch: 什么叫「绿色通道纯粹」配色啦！唔姆…不过好像确实只有绿色也会很单调，而且容易让人无法辨识紧急出口的位置。
t_en: That's not the name of the colorway! Well, using only green color might be monotonous and make it difficult for passengers to locate the emergency exit.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客的评价确实公平公正，只是通过给配色取外号的方式，就凸显出了其中的核心问题！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you've indeed evaluated the colorways fairly. The nicknames you gave them have highlighted their core weaknesses!

t_ch: 看来帕姆的列车灯光配色方案待调优的空间还很大啊…哎。
t_en: Seems like there's a lot of room for improvement in my lighting plans... *sigh*

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，你有没有感觉…最近我有什么变化？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, do you notice anything different about me lately?

t_ch: 变得更加圆润可爱了。
t_en: You look rounder and more adorable.

t_ch: 圆…圆润？！帕姆不想变成像是黑米团子一样的东西！
t_en: R—Rounder!? I don't want to look like a chocolate doughnut!

t_ch: 变得更加憨态可掬了。
t_en: You look friendlier and more approachable.

t_ch: 总…总觉得听上去怪怪的……
t_en: Wh—Why does it sound a bit weird to me...

t_ch: 果然不是幻觉…帕姆最近的确是胖了！本来还以为是衣服缩水导致的，结果试了新的衣服也是一样。
t_en: So it's not my illusion... I've gained weight! At first, I thought it was because my shirt shrunk, but I still felt tight after putting on new clothes.

t_ch: 呜呜，甚至就连在列车上打扫卫生过久，也会感觉特别疲劳，喘不上气。
t_en: *sob* I also feel tired and get breathless easily when I'm cleaning the coaches longer than usual.

t_ch: 影响身体健康可不好。
t_en: You should take care of your health.

t_ch: 确实如此…身体才是担任列车长的本钱！
t_en: That's right... Being healthy is crucial for a conductor!

t_ch: 会变成黑米团子的。
t_en: You'll turn into a chocolate doughnut.

t_ch: 不要哇！那样的话跃迁的时候很容易发生危险事故的！
t_en: Oh, no! It'll be dangerous for me during a Warp!

t_ch: 事到如今，只有一个方法…那就是锻炼身体！至于锻炼的方法，之前有在书里看到过！一定没问题的！
t_en: Now, there's only one way to solve this — exercise! I've read about some techniques in books. I'm sure I can do it!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，你就陪陪帕姆好吗？放心放心，不会累到你的！
t_en: Passenger {NICKNAME}, can you stay here with me? Don't worry. I won't tire you out!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，我已经做好准备了，接下来就辛苦你听一下规则吧！
t_en: Passenger {NICKNAME}, I'm ready. Now, please bear with me as I explain the rules to you!

t_ch: 书上说，进行全身有氧运动是最适合的，而在列车中最方便的有氧运动，就是跑步啦！
t_en: According to the book, full-body aerobic exercise is the most suitable for me. Jogging is the easiest sport I can do on the Express!

t_ch: 但是单纯地跑步难免有些单调，所以帕姆选择趣味跑步法！喏，这个球球你拿着。
t_en: Yet, jogging can be boring, so I've decided to use a fun jogging method! Here, take this ball.

t_ch: 接下来，{NICKNAME}乘客就负责把球球扔出去，然后帕姆把球球捡回来！一来一回，也算是有氧锻炼啦！
t_en: Now, {NICKNAME}, I need you to throw the ball out for me, and I'll retrieve it! Doing a few rounds of this can be considered good aerobic exercise!

t_ch: 这个我熟。
t_en: I'm familiar with this.

t_ch: 那就太好啦！
t_en: That's great!

t_ch: 总感觉怪怪的…
t_en: There's something odd about the method...

t_ch: 很简单的规则，试一试就会很快上手的，放心吧！
t_en: The rules are very simple — I'm sure you'll quickly get the hang of it!

t_ch: 现在就把球球扔出去吧！帕姆已经做好准备啦！
t_en: Throw the ball now! I'm ready!

t_ch: 你将球球扔了出去，帕姆很快捡了回来。如此这般重复了许多次……
t_en: You throw the ball, and Pom-Pom gets it back quickly. The process is repeated many times...

t_ch: 感觉有点累了，呼呼…稍微休息一下帕！
t_en: I'm a little tired, huff... Let's take a break!

t_ch: 乖乖帕姆，做得好！
t_en: Well done, Pom-Pom! Great job!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，毕竟我可是列车长！诶不对…总感觉你说的话怪怪的。
t_en: Hee-hee, don't forget I'm the conductor! Wait a minute... Your comment sounds odd to me.

t_ch: 比佩佩还要灵活呢。
t_en: You're more agile than Peppy.

t_ch: 下次可以把那个叫佩佩的家伙叫过来，咱们比一比就知道了！诶不对…总感觉佩佩这个名字我听过。
t_en: You should invite Peppy over next time to see who's better! Wait a minute... The name rings a bell.

t_ch: 我知道了！你是把我和狗狗作比较了对吧！{NICKNAME}乘客太过分了！
t_en: I get it! You're comparing me to a dog! {NICKNAME}, you're awful!

t_ch: 这可是很科学的有氧锻炼方法！帕姆可是在书里看到的！不信你自己搜，书名是《成为人类好朋友的<unbreak>100</unbreak>种方法》！
t_en: This is a scientifically proven aerobic exercise I learned from a book! If you don't believe me, you can read it yourself! The title of the book is 100 Ways to Be a Good Friend to Humans.

t_ch: 诶…人类的好朋友…啊！原来一直是这个意思…
t_en: Hmm... A good friend to humans... Oh! The book's meant for dogs...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，请注意抬脚！帕姆正在打扫卫生！
t_en: Passenger {NICKNAME}, please keep your legs up! I'm cleaning the floor!

t_ch: 也不用每天都打扫吧？
t_en: Is it necessary to clean the floor every day?

t_ch: 地板都快反光了…
t_en: The floor is turning into a mirror...

t_ch: 细菌都是看不见的！空气中有那么多浮尘，日积月累下来就会有一层灰了！
t_en: Bacteria are not visible to the human eye! There are many dust particles in the air! There will be a layer of dust after a while.

t_ch: 尤其是现在…车厢里的湿度这么低，空气这么干燥，如果不多加打扫的话，乘客也是很容易生病的。
t_en: Now that the humidity in the coaches is low and the air is dry, cleaning is especially important, or the passengers will fall sick easily.

t_ch: 要是可以有谁帮帮帕姆就好了…你说是吧，{NICKNAME}乘客？
t_en: It'd be great if someone could help me... Don't you think so, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 我说不是吧…
t_en: I don't think so...

t_ch: 怎么会是呢？
t_en: How can it be?

t_ch: 哼，可别想跑！列车长已经选定你啦！过来帮我搭把手吧！加湿器还没修好，只能手动洒水了。
t_en: Hmph, don't think of running away, as I have chosen you! Please give me a hand! As the humidifier is still broken, I have to resort to sprinkling water manually.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，要注意，洒太多不行，洒太少也不行哦！
t_en: Passenger {NICKNAME}, please make sure you sprinkle just the right amount of water!

t_ch: 等你洒好水了，帕姆就来拖地，在此之前我就先休息一下帕……
t_en: After you've done that, I'll mop the floor. I'll rest while waiting for you...

t_ch: 你注意到列车的通风系统正在运作，你决定多洒些水，以免蒸发得太快。
t_en: Noticing that the ventilation system is working, you decide to sprinkle more water so that it doesn't evaporate too quickly.

t_ch: 诶，{NICKNAME}乘客就已经搞定了吗？呜…帕姆才刚刚开始做美梦呢！
t_en: Eh, {NICKNAME}, have you finished sprinkling already? Ugh... I've just started a nice dream!

t_ch: 让我检查一下…呜哇！怎么地板这么湿了！难道{NICKNAME}乘客把水壶打翻了吗？
t_en: Let me check... Woah! Why is the floor so wet? Did you flip over a kettle or something, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 呜呜，这下子清扫起来可就麻烦了…不过也怪帕姆没有给你说清楚，剩下的工作就交给我帕……
t_en: Oh, no! Cleaning this up will be a lot of work... It's my fault for not giving you clear instructions. Just leave the rest to me...

t_ch: 哎，打扫起来就没完没了，要是能解放双手就好了……
t_en: Ugh, so much cleaning to do. If only I could free my hands...

t_ch: 这不就是列车长的工作？
t_en: Isn't cleaning the job of the conductor?

t_ch: 列车长的工作可不仅仅是保洁这么简单。
t_en: The conductor's job is more than that.

t_ch: 就当是在锻炼身体了。
t_en: Just think of it as exercising.

t_ch: 想不到{NICKNAME}乘客还挺会苦中作乐的。
t_en: You sure do know how to make the best out of a bad situation.

t_ch: 「双手」？
t_en: Hands?

t_ch: 这位乘客，你什么眼神？！
t_en: What's that look in your eyes?

t_ch: 听说艾丝妲给空间站买了处理杂务的机器人，要是能借一两个到列车上来就好了。
t_en: I heard that Asta bought some robots for the space station to handle chores. It would be great if I could borrow one or two for the Express.

t_ch: 对了，{NICKNAME}乘客，你和空间站关系不是挺好的吗？既然你现在有空，帮我去问问吧？
t_en: By the way, passenger {NICKNAME}, aren't you close to the space station? Since you are free now, would you do me a favor and go borrow some robots for me?

t_ch: 实在找不到相关负责人的话…直接问艾丝妲也行！就说是「列车长的请求」！
t_en: If you really can't find the person in charge... You can just ask Asta directly! Just say it's a request from the conductor!

t_ch: 帕姆想借小机器人…
t_en: Pom-Pom wants to borrow some little robots...

t_ch: 诶，帕姆想要小机器人吗？啊，我上次的确说过这事……
t_en: Oh, they do? Ah, I did mention the robots to them that last time...

t_ch: 不过…这批机器人虽然是我定制的，但总感觉不太好用。在科员们的满意度调查里，它的得分很低。
t_en: Well... Although these robots are customized by me, they don't feel very good to use and scored very low in the staff satisfaction survey.

t_ch: 哎，{NICKNAME}，麻烦你跟帕姆解释清楚吧，不是不借，实在是不好意思坑你们。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, please tell Pom-Pom, it's not that I don't want to lend them, it's just...

t_ch: 我想借小机器人…
t_en: I want to borrow some little robots...

t_ch: 痴迷这种劣质机械的多少有点…咳咳…你是来聊工作上的事啊，抱歉抱歉。
t_en: People who are obsessed with this kind of inferior machinery are a bit... Ahem... Oh, you are here to talk about work, sorry.

t_ch: 你说这种小机器人，艾丝妲站长之前就下了命令，让我收回所有机器人，准备联络售后呢。
t_en: You mean this kind of robot? Asta has ordered me to withdraw all of them and contact the after-sales service.

t_ch: 这玩意儿总是时不时自动关机，要不然就是导航报错。我研究过了，它的底层逻辑一塌糊涂……
t_en: These little robots always shut down automatically from time to time, and also report errors in navigation. I've researched them, and their underlying logic is a mess...

t_ch: 总之我劝你还是打消这个念头算了。
t_en: It's best not to use them.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，机器人的事情有着落了吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, how's the robot thing going?

t_ch: （将事情的原委告诉帕姆）
t_en: (Tells Pom-Pom about the robots)

t_ch: 哎…看来是帕姆的运气不太好……
t_en: *sigh* It seems that Pom-Pom doesn't have the best luck...

t_ch: 算了，就算没有小机器人，帕姆也能搞的定！
t_en: Forget it. Pom-Pom can handle this even without those robots!

t_ch: ——毕竟眼前的大活人可比机器人方便多了！
t_en: After all, a living person is much better than a robot!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，喏，扫把给你！都是列车上的一份子，替列车长分忧也是应该的嘛！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, take the broom! As part of the Express, you should share the worries of the conductor when the time comes!

t_ch: 我还有事先走了。
t_en: I have other things to do. See you later.

t_ch: 这个重任我承担不起。
t_en: I am busy with something else.

t_ch: 我就知道{NICKNAME}乘客会这么说！
t_en: Pom-Pom knew you would say that!

t_ch: 算了，你去忙你的吧！这种事情我一个人也能解决！
t_en: Forget it. Go and do your work! Pom-Pom can handle this kind of thing alone!

t_ch: 为了下次的冒险…要保证充足的补给，就得要……
t_en: For the next adventure... To ensure sufficient supplies, you have to...

t_ch: 我将带头削减开支…
t_en: I will lead the way in cutting spending...

t_ch: 想不到{NICKNAME}乘客还有这样奉献自我的决心，帕姆很高兴！
t_en: Can't believe you have such altruistic dedication, {NICKNAME}. Pom-Pom is very happy!

t_ch: 不过你不用担心，一切都在帕姆的计算之中。
t_en: But don't worry, everything is under Pom-Pom's control.

t_ch: 派我出去打猎？
t_en: You want to send me out hunting?

t_ch: 你在想什么奇怪的事？这可是星穹列车帕！
t_en: What are you thinking? This is the Astral Express!

t_ch: 把你做成备用粮！
t_en: I'll just make you our emergency food.

t_ch: 你听听你说的还是人话吗？！怎么可以这么对待你的列车长！
t_en: Listen to yourself! How could you do that to your conductor?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客可能还不了解帕！为了保证星穹列车的正常运转，需要准备的物资五花八门……
t_en: You may not know this, but various materials are needed in order to ensure the normal operation of the Astral Express...

t_ch: 不过之前我们和空间站交情不错，姬子和艾丝妲达成了某种「资源补给协议」。
t_en: But before we had a good relationship with the space station, Himeko and Asta reached some kind of resource supply agreement.

t_ch: 一些基础物资可以直接去找对应科室要～当然，太过贵重和麻烦的东西还是不要麻烦他们了。
t_en: For some basic supplies, we can go directly to the corresponding department to ask for them. Well, but don't ask for things that are too expensive and troublesome.

t_ch: 正好{NICKNAME}乘客在，我跟艾丝妲打过招呼，她已经知会过对应科室的负责人了，就麻烦你代我跑一趟帕！
t_en: Since you are here, I have told Asta, and she has already informed the person in charge of the corresponding department, why don't you run the errand for me this time!

t_ch: 关于「资源补给协议」…
t_en: About the resource supply agreement...

t_ch: 噢，正巧「种子岛」新研发了一批代餐食品。稍等，我需要在终端上走一下流程。
t_en: Oh, it just so happens that the Seed Isle has newly developed a batch of meal replacement foods. Hold on, I need to get the process done on the terminal.

t_ch: 找到了，请在这里签一下字。因为是代理领取资源补给，需要把你和委托人的名字都写上。
t_en: Found it. Please sign here. Since you are taking the resource supplies for someone else, you need to write down both your name and your client's name.

t_ch: 代理人{NICKNAME}，委托人帕姆。好的，识别通过。之后我会安排科员把你们需要的东西运输过去。
t_en: Agent: {NICKNAME}, Consignor: Pom-Pom. OK, identification passed. I will arrange for staff to transport the things you need for you later.

t_ch: 关于「资源补给协议」…
t_en: About the resource supply agreement...

t_ch: 「资源补给协议」…哦哦！艾丝妲站长之前和我说过！这点小事，明德肯定给你办妥，只要小友日后别忘了明德就好！
t_en: Resource supply agreement... Oh right! Lead Researcher Asta told me about it before! I'll get this trivial matter done for you, as long as you don't forget this favor in the future!

t_ch: 之后我会安排科员把东西送过去的，就不劳烦小友亲自搬运了！
t_en: I will arrange for staff to deliver the things, so you don't need to carry them yourself!

t_ch: 关于「资源补给协议」…
t_en: About the resource supply agreement...

t_ch: 哼，我们地概科的资源都这么紧缺了，你还好意思来找我们拿东西？
t_en: Hmph, we at the Department of Cosmic Geography are also short on resources. Where do you find the nerve to come to us for things?

t_ch: 等等…「资源补给协议」…艾丝妲站长好像说过这事，那没事了。
t_en: Wait... The resource supply agreement... Lead Researcher Asta seems to have mentioned this.

t_ch: 别在这儿干等着了，户外勘测的装备，我一把老骨头还能给你现在就拿过来不成？待会儿我会叫个小年轻给你直接送到月台！
t_en: Don't just wait here. Are you expecting me, an old man, to bring the equipment for an outdoor survey to you? Later, I will get a youngster to send them directly to the railway platform for you!

t_ch: 关于「资源补给协议」…
t_en: About the resource supply agreement...

t_ch: 哦…我知道了，是和星穹列车相关的协议吧？我们密卷科的确弄到了一批有意思的文献……
t_en: Oh... I see, it's an agreement related to the Astral Express, right? The Department of Insight has indeed obtained a batch of interesting documents...

t_ch: 不过我现在有点事走不开，我待会儿会让人把资料给你们传输过去的。
t_en: But I have something to do right now, I'll have someone transfer the information to you later.

t_ch: 关于「资源补给协议」…
t_en: About the resource supply agreement...

t_ch: 哦，想不到真的有一天会启动这项协议。毕竟咱们法政科，能提供的资源不算多。
t_en: Oh, never imagined that this agreement would actually be activated one day. After all, we at the Department of Galactic Geopolitics do not have many resources to offer.

t_ch: 好了，年轻人，审批流程中涉及我这边权限的，都已经通过了。之后会有专门负责人完成资源交接的。
t_en: Alright, everything that involves my authority in the approval process has been passed. There will be a person to take care of the handover of resources later.

t_ch: 关于「资源补给协议」…
t_en: About the resource supply agreement...

t_ch: 哈…医疗补给申请需要静候三到五个工作日，我们会以邮件的形式进行答复……
t_en: Ha... The application for medical supplies needs to wait for three to five working days, and we will contact you through mail...

t_ch: 等等，「资源补给协议」？哦，我想起来了，那个走特殊流程的啊。
t_en: Wait, resource supply agreement? Oh, I remember, the one that requires a special procedure.

t_ch: 好了，流程审批已经搞定，之后会有对应人员送过去的，你回去等着吧。哈欠——
t_en: Well, the approval process has been completed, and the corresponding personnel will send it over later. You can go back and wait now. *yawns*

t_ch: 关于「资源补给协议」…
t_en: About the resource supply agreement...

t_ch: 和之前一样，常用零件都给你们准备好了，一会儿就安排小机器人给你们配送过去。
t_en: As before, the common parts are ready, and a little robot will be arranged to deliver them to you in a while.

t_ch: 对了，到时候记得给我们的机器人配送服务打个好评啊！
t_en: By the way, remember to give our robot's delivery service a good review!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}乘客，你也是来听歌的吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you also here to listen to music?

t_ch: 不行吗？
t_en: Is there a problem?

t_ch: 留声机摆在这儿，就是给大家听歌用的，当然欢迎。
t_en: The Phonograph is here for everyone to listen to music, of course you are welcome to use it.

t_ch: 我只是看看你在干嘛。
t_en: I just want to see what you're doing.

t_ch: 我来检查下留声机，顺便听听歌。
t_en: I am here to check the Phonograph and listen to the music.

t_ch: 可惜这里没有我喜欢的歌…
t_en: It's a pity that there is no song I like here...

t_ch: 留声机是用碟片的，想听什么你自己找不就好了。
t_en: The Phonograph uses discs. Just find the disc of the song you want to hear.

t_ch: 现在列车上收藏的碟片并不多，你要是有什么特别想听的音乐，可以先跟我说。
t_en: There are not many discs on the train now. If you have any music you want to listen to in particular, you can tell Pom-Pom.

t_ch: 我想听点摇滚乐…
t_en: I want to listen to some rock music...

t_ch: 摇滚啊，帕姆对摇滚了解的不多，你要是有什么精于此道的朋友，可以问问他们能不能给你搞到几张经典专辑？
t_en: Rock... Pom-Pom doesn't know much about rock. If you have any friends who love rock music, you can ask them if they can get you some classic albums?

t_ch: 这样的朋友…我还真认识！
t_en: I do know one!

t_ch: 只要有碟片，一切都好说。
t_en: As long as they have discs.

t_ch: 嗯？怎么了？
t_en: Hmm? What's up?

t_ch: （告知碟片的事）
t_en: (Tell her about the discs)

t_ch: 摇滚？那你可问对人了，我们乐队…啊不对，说到专辑的话，唔…恐怕不行。
t_en: Rock music? You've come to the right person. Our band... ah no. I'm afraid I can't help you.

t_ch: 为什么不行？
t_en: Why?

t_ch: 哎，说来话长了，这和乐队的成员变动有关。
t_en: It's a long story. Our band is undergoing some changes.

t_ch: 我可以花钱买！
t_en: I can pay you!

t_ch: 和钱没有关系，我要是有就直接送你了！
t_en: It's not about money. If I have any discs, I will give them to you directly!

t_ch: 怎么说呢，以前是有发售的，不过数量不多，贝洛伯格就这么大，平时有巡演就够了。
t_en: How should I put it... We used to sell our albums, though the quantity was not that large. Belobog is not that big, so usually a tour is enough.

t_ch: 后来嘛…有些人员变化——邓恩的事你是知道的。再还有贝斯手这些老大难的问题……
t_en: Then... There were some personnel changes, you know with Dunn... Then there's the perennial problem of our bass player...

t_ch: 我正在考虑要不要重录以前的专辑呢，所以碟片什么的，我现在可给不了你。
t_en: I'm considering re-recording the previous album, so I can't give you the disc right now.

t_ch: 那好吧…
t_en: Okay then...

t_ch: 尊重，理解！
t_en: I understand that.

t_ch: 抱歉啦，{NICKNAME}！以后等我处理完乐队的事情，有机会的话我肯定会送你几张专辑的！
t_en: Sorry, {NICKNAME}. When I finish handling the band affairs, I will definitely send you some albums if I have the chance!

t_ch: 禁止乘客躺在沙发上吃东西…禁止乘客在帕姆刚拖干净的地板上踩出脚印…禁止……
t_en: No eating on the couch... No footprints on Pom-Pom's freshly mopped floor... No...

t_ch: 不如禁止乘客上列车。
t_en: What about no passengers on the Express.

t_ch: 禁止乘客上列车…不对！列车上没有乘客了，那还叫什么列车！
t_en: No passengers on the Express... No passengers on the train!? Then what kind of train is that!

t_ch: 你这规矩也太多了吧！
t_en: You have too many rules!

t_ch: 诶？有吗！我一直闷着头写准则，自己还没来得及仔细看看呢！
t_en: Really? I have been writing these guidelines, and I haven't had time to take a closer look at them!

t_ch: 哎，现在列车上的乘客越来越多，什么人都有——呃，我不是在说你，不是每个乘客都像你一样乖巧懂事。
t_en: There are more and more passengers on the train now, and there are all sorts around. Pom-Pom is not talking about you. Not every passenger is as well-behaved and sensible as you.

t_ch: 帕姆在想，身为列车长，接下来得加强对列车的管控，这需要管理学知识……
t_en: Pom-Pom is thinking, as the conductor, Pom-Pom needs to strengthen my control of the train, which requires knowledge of management...

t_ch: 对了，{NICKNAME}乘客，我听三月七乘客说，贝洛伯格可是个大城市，你们还认识那里的大人物！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I heard from March 7th that Belobog is a big city and you know the bigwigs there!

t_ch: 想必会有精通于此的人帕！你有什么推荐的人选吗？
t_en: Some of them must be proficient in management! Do you have any recommendations for candidates?

t_ch: 诚挚向你推荐上层区的…
t_en: I would recommend the Overworld's...

t_ch: 佩拉。
t_en: Pela.

t_ch: 杰帕德。
t_en: Gepard.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅。
t_en: Bronya.

t_ch: 我再想想…
t_en: Let me think...

t_ch: 诚挚向你推荐下层区的…
t_en: I would recommend the Underworld's...

t_ch: 虎克。
t_en: Hook.

t_ch: 娜塔莎。
t_en: Natasha.

t_ch: 史瓦罗和克拉拉。
t_en: Svarog and Clara.

t_ch: 我再想想…
t_en: Let me think...

t_ch: 我就说嘛，你果然是在贝洛伯格认识了不少新朋友，那就麻烦你代我去请教一二帕！
t_en: As expected, you have met many new friends in Belobog. I would like to trouble you to reach out for advice for me!

t_ch: 请教管理经验…
t_en: Please give me some management advice...

t_ch: 管理经验？你很有眼色嘛！
t_en: Management? You've come to the right person!

t_ch: 记住组织里的每个成员，还要带大家一起吃好吃的，玩好玩的！还有…还有…定期组织大探险活动！
t_en: Remembering every member in The Moles, bringing everyone to eat delicious food, and having fun together! And... and... conducting regular big adventures!

t_ch: 这样才能当好鼹鼠党的老大！怎么样，虎克是不是很厉害！一般人可做不到呢！
t_en: That's how you become the boss of The Moles! Am I amazing or what! Ordinary people can't do it!

t_ch: 请教管理经验…
t_en: Please give me some management advice...

t_ch: 这其实和情报官的工作一样，都要依靠脑力。
t_en: In fact, management is the same as the work of an intelligence officer, and it all depends on brain power.

t_ch: 细心地搜集每个人的资料，梳理出有用的部分，根据实际情况分门别类备份整理…基本就是这些了。
t_en: Carefully collect everyone's information, sort out the useful parts, and organize them according to the actual situation... Basically, that's it.

t_ch: 听起来虽然很庞杂，但实际做起来会很有成就感。诀窍的话，就是细致。
t_en: It sounds complicated, but doing it can be very rewarding. The trick is to be meticulous.

t_ch: 请教管理经验…
t_en: Please give me some management advice...

t_ch: 我最近也在思考这方面的内容。你要是问从前的我，可能会得到无数个听上去很专业的回答。
t_en: I've been thinking about this as well recently. If you ask the old me, you may get countless professional-sounding answers.

t_ch: 但现在，我的答案只有一个——
t_en: But now, I have only one answer for you.

t_ch: 要站在大家的角度切身处地去思考。因为我的每一个决策，都可能影响到无数人的生活……
t_en: Think from everyone's point of view. Because every decision I make may affect the lives of countless people...

t_ch: 请教管理经验…
t_en: Please give me some management advice...

t_ch: 管理经验？这些年来我在「地火」的确积累了一些心得，不过也称不上什么经验——那就是把专业的事情交给专业的人去做。
t_en: Management? Over the years, I have accumulated some experience in Wildfire, but it can't really be called management. I just leave professional things to professional people.

t_ch: 举个例子，下层区的秩序过去是「地火」来维护，但以后我们会交还到银鬃铁卫手里，毕竟他们才是专业人士。
t_en: For example, the order in the Underworld used to be maintained by Wildfire, but in the future we will hand it back to the Silvermane Guards. After all, they are professionals.

t_ch: 有他们在，我们才能腾得出手，去处理其他事务。这样一来，下层区的居民，既能同时得到铁卫的保护，也能得到我们的帮助。
t_en: With them, we can be free to deal with other affairs. In this way, the Underworld people can not only get the protection of the Silvermane Guards, but also get our help.

t_ch: 请教管理经验…
t_en: Please give me some management advice...

t_ch: 这个问题…得请教史瓦罗先生。
t_en: This question... I have to ask Mr. Svarog.

t_ch: 正在处理「外来者」的提问。
t_en: Question from the outsider is being processed.

t_ch: 计算，需要按照既定的规则，将一切可能与概率演算出来，执行风险最低与成功率最高的结果。
t_en: Calculations require consideration of all possibilities and probabilities according to the established rules, and executing the result with the lowest risk and the highest success rate.

t_ch: 请教管理经验…
t_en: Please give me some management advice...

t_ch: 要管理列车…大概就是要有一定的觉悟。
t_en: Managing a train... probably requires a certain amount of awareness.

t_ch: 要负责列车乘客的日常生活，事项繁琐又枯燥，那就得有勇气，随时准备好承担一切职责。
t_en: You will be responsible for the daily life of the passengers, which is tedious and boring, so you have to have courage and be ready to take on all responsibilities at any time.

t_ch: 必要时，连休息时间都要贡献出来，得有这样的觉悟才行。
t_en: When necessary, you need to contribute even your rest time. This kind of awareness is required.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，快告诉帕姆，你都问到了什么吧！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, tell Pom-Pom what advice you got!

t_ch: （将管理秘诀告诉帕姆）
t_en: (Tell Pom-Pom the management advice)

t_ch: 哦！帕姆好像懂了。但…又好像没懂？这就是管理学大师的建议帕，很有深度！
t_en: Oh! Pom-Pom gets it. But... Pom-Pom is not sure... Advice from the master of management is truly deep!

t_ch: 要制定列车行为准则，帕姆还有很长的路要走呢。
t_en: Pom-Pom still has a long way to go to develop a code of conduct for the Express.

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 我去空间站问过了…
t_en: I asked around in the space station...

t_ch: 你怎么心事重重的样子？
t_en: What's weighing on your mind?

t_ch: 列车长在这儿干什么呢？
t_en: What are you doing here, conductor?

t_ch: 有些事想请教下你…
t_en: I wanna ask you something...

t_ch: 我回来咯！
t_en: I'm back!

t_ch: 我打听到了！
t_en: I got the news!

t_ch: 列车设施
t_en: Express Facilities

t_ch: 关于丹恒：睡觉
t_en: About Dan Heng: Sleep

t_ch: 丹恒乘客睡在资料室里，我以前查房的时候，还有些担心他就这么睡觉会不会睡不着和着凉什么的……
t_en: Passenger Dan Heng sleeps in the archives. I don't know how he manages to get a good night's rest in that room, though. I was always worrying that he'll catch a cold.

t_ch: 不过后来帕姆发现，服务器机组散发的热量还是挺暖和的。而且丹恒乘客对此完全不在意，让帕姆不要担心他。
t_en: But then Pom-Pom made a discovery: The heat emanating from the server unit provides plenty of warmth. And since Dan Heng himself insists on sleeping there, Pom-Pom decided to stop worrying about him so much.

t_ch: 他还说什么自己在绳子上也能睡得着。唔，你说这是什么原理，真是奇怪……
t_en: He also said something about being able to sleep on the rope. Pom-Pom don't know what he meant by that, seems really strange if you ask me...

t_ch: 关于丹恒：姬子的咖啡
t_en: About Dan Heng: Himeko's coffee

t_ch: 帕姆最佩服丹恒乘客的一点是，他居然喝得下姬子的咖啡，甚至还称赞过几次。
t_en: One of the things Pom-Pom admires about Dan Heng is that he can actually drink Himeko's coffee. He even complimented her on it a few times.

t_ch: 我一度怀疑丹恒乘客的舌头是不是有什么问题…不过他偶然跟我透露过，原来他认为姬子的咖啡是用来锤炼心性的绝佳工具。
t_en: I used to wonder if there was something wrong with Dan Heng's taste buds... but later he mentioned to me in passing that he regards Himeko's coffee as an excellent tool to test his grit.

t_ch: …总之这件事你可不要告诉姬子帕，姬子身为列车的领航员，没有功劳也有苦劳，总要给她留几分薄面的……
t_en: ...Anyway, please don't mention any of this to Himeko. As the Express' navigator, she puts in a lot of work that goes unnoticed, so Pom-Pom tries to not be too hard on her.

t_ch: 关于姬子：姬子的咖啡手艺
t_en: About Himeko: Himeko's hand-blended coffee

t_ch: 帕姆不明白姬子明明有精湛的维修技艺，为什么却成天捣鼓些不着调的「手调咖啡」。
t_en: Himeko is highly competent when it comes to fixing things, but then she's always pumping out this uninspired "hand-blended coffee." Pom-Pom just doesn't get it.

t_ch: 你说她的咖啡是「黑泥」对吧？哎，其实这已经是她改良过的版本了，现在好歹是用单分子锯手动打磨的咖啡豆。
t_en: What was the word you used to describe her coffee — "black sludge," was it? Well as it turns out, this is already the new and improved version of her coffee: The coffee beans are polished by hand with a monomolecular blade.

t_ch: 要还是以前啊，她用的还是速溶咖啡粉，那个剂量…那才是真正的「黑泥」，完全是一团糊糊嘛！
t_en: Back in the old days, she would just use instant coffee powder. The amount of powder she used gave a whole new meaning to the phrase "black sludge."

t_ch: 关于瓦尔特杨：尴尬的过去
t_en: About Welt Yang: An embarrassing past

t_ch: 瓦尔特…瓦尔特是个稳重的人，似乎经历了很多，不过帕姆曾见过他少有的失态的样子。
t_en: Welt... Welt is a man who seems to have been through a lot. He is calm and composed, though Pom-Pom has seen him lose his cool on rare occasions.

t_ch: 唔？你想问什么会失态？哦，其实也没什么，瓦尔特乘客以前是位动画师帕，列车经过某个星球的时候，他用「拟态」能力复现了其中几集给别人看。
t_en: Hmm? You want to know what caused him to lose his cool? Oh, it's nothing really. You see, Welt used to be an animator. Whenever the Express passed by certain planets, he would use his "mimicry" ability to reproduce a few episodes for others to see.

t_ch: 结果那几个观众追着问他要续集，瓦尔特乘客就躲在列车里一连几天没下车，说是帮姬子修理列车上的什么东西了。
t_en: This resulted in a group of fans chasing after him to beg for a sequel. Welt hid in the Express for several days straight, saying that he was just helping Himeko do some repairs.

t_ch: 不过，帕姆怎么不记得当时列车上有什么设备要修呢？
t_en: But Pom-Pom doesn't remember the Express needing any kind of repairs at the time...

t_ch: 关于瓦尔特杨：瞌睡趣事
t_en: About Welt: Sleeping story

t_ch: 瓦尔特乘客以前是位动画师，他好像对自己的作品十分自豪帕。闲着的时候，也会主动使用「拟态」能力复现出来播给大家看。
t_en: Welt used to be an animator, and he seems to be very proud of his work. Whenever things got slow and everyone had some downtime, Welt would use his "mimicry" ability to re-broadcast his show for everyone.

t_ch: 不过有好几次，帕姆都看到他明明是睡着了，但「拟态」还在不断循环播放动画。
t_en: On several occasions, Pom-Pom caught Welt sleeping in the middle of the broadcast!

t_ch: 我问他要不要关掉舱灯和「拟态」，让他回房间好好睡一觉。但他却坚持说他没有睡着，「拟态」和灯都别关，说什么他还要看的。
t_en: I asked him if he wanted to turn off the cabin lights and his mimicry broadcast so he could go to his room and get a good night's sleep. But he insisted that he didn't fall asleep, and that the show must go on.

t_ch: 什么嘛，帕姆明明看到他都打呼噜了。
t_en: But who does he think he's fooling? Pom-Pom saw him fall asleep right in front of me.

t_ch: 关于三月七：帕姆的辫子
t_en: About March 7th: Pom-Pom's ears

t_ch: 三月乘客啊，是最令帕姆头疼的乘客，她总想用帕姆的耳朵绑辫子戴蝴蝶结，真是可怕……
t_en: Out of all the passengers on the Express, March 7th causes the most headaches for me. She always wants to tie my ears into bows...

t_ch: 所以帕姆只要看到三月乘客开着相机，帕姆就会想办法去做别的事。而且帕姆也是真的很忙啦。
t_en: So whenever I see March with her camera in hand, Pom-Pom will find an excuse to go do something else. Pom-Pom's always really busy, after all.

t_ch: 关于三月七：果汁爱好者
t_en: About March 7th: Juice connoisseur

t_ch: 身为星穹列车的列车长，为每位乘客提供一流的服务自然是我的职责。
t_en: As the conductor of the Express, it is naturally my duty to provide first-class service to every passenger.

t_ch: 像三月乘客那样要求提供无限畅饮的果汁，这种事情自然也难不倒我。
t_en: A request for "unlimited juice" — put forward by passenger March — does not even pose the slightest challenge for a conductor such as myself.

t_ch: 因为三月乘客对口味也不挑剔，好像只要是果汁她就特别喜欢喝……
t_en: March doesn't seem to be particular about taste. As long as it's juice, she'll like it.

t_ch: 关于三月七：少女的心事
t_en: About March 7th: A young girl's heart

t_ch: 别看三月乘客成天吵吵闹闹的，就算没人跟她讲话，她自己和自己就能聊起来。
t_en: Don't think that March is running around and chatting all day long just because she has company. Even when she's alone, she'll strike up a conversation with herself.

t_ch: 但在整理相册和写日记的时候，她好像会格外地安静。有时候帕姆会看到她望向窗外出神，也不知道她到底在想什么帕。
t_en: March does seem to quiet down suddenly whenever she's organizing photo albums or writing in her diary. Sometimes Pom-Pom sees her gazing out the window. Pom-Pom wonders what she's thinking about.

t_ch: 关于列车：列车设施
t_en: About the Express: Facilities

t_ch: 列车上有不同的房间，自然就有不同的设施。
t_en: There are different rooms on the Express, so naturally there are different facilities as well.

t_ch: 像卫生间这种东西当然存在啦，不过通常在大家过量饮用餐车饮料后，就会要排队。
t_en: Of course the Express has things like bathrooms, but when all the passengers overindulge on drinks from the food truck, they have to wait in line to use them.

t_ch: 还有…为什么大家总是把手机丢在卫生间的洗手台上呢？真是奇怪啊，我总是能在那儿捡到大家的手机。
t_en: And... why does everyone always leave their phones on the bathroom counter? It's so weird, I'm always finding people's phones there.

t_ch: 关于帕姆：帕姆的故事
t_en: About Pom-Pom: Pom-Pom's story

t_ch: 我是谁，我的过去？你问这些有什么意义吗？
t_en: Who am I and what is my past? Is there any point in you asking about such things?

t_ch: 帕姆就是帕姆，帕姆是你们尊敬的列车长大人，在列车上要遵守列车的规矩，不要总是惹帕姆生气。
t_en: Pom-Pom is Pom-Pom, your respectful conductor. Follow the rules of the Express, and try not make me angry all the time.

t_ch: 关于帕姆：列车与旅途
t_en: About Pom-Pom: The Express and its journeys

t_ch: 外面的世界啊…帕姆的确是很想看看外面…不过，旅途的意义并不在于外面的风景有多么精彩，而是在于与你同行的人。
t_en: The outside world... Pom-Pom does want to see it. However, the meaning of the journey lies not in how wonderful the scenery is outside, but in the people you travel with.

t_ch: 无论是帕姆还是身为乘客的你们，要珍惜和我们一起共同乘车的同伴…这样的话，那片刻的欢乐与感情就有了更深刻的意义……
t_en: Whether it's me or passengers such as yourself, it's the same for all of us — we need to cherish the companions who ride along with us... That will make the moments of joy and bonding along the way even more meaningful...

t_ch: 关于丹恒：不愉快的梦
t_en: About Dan Heng: Bad dreams

t_ch: 说起来，丹恒乘客时常通宵整理资料，睡眠质量很成问题帕。
t_en: Come to think of it, passenger Dan Heng often spends entire nights organizing files. I imagine his sleep quality isn't great.

t_ch: 有些日子，帕姆为他送去餐点时，他似乎都做了不愉快的梦，偶尔还会被帕姆惊醒。
t_en: Some days, when I went to bring him his meal, it seemed that he was having bad dreams. Sometimes I even wake him up.

t_ch: 丹恒乘客到底梦见了什么呢？…啊，窥探乘客的隐私可不是列车长该做的事情。
t_en: Pom-Pom wonders, what is Dan Heng dreaming about? ...Ah, it is not proper for a conductor to pry on a passenger's private affairs...

t_ch: 关于丹恒：长枪
t_en: About Dan Heng: The spear

t_ch: 丹恒乘客时常会用心保养擦拭他的长枪。虽然列车很安全，但他似乎还是没有放下防范之心。
t_en: Passenger Dan Heng often carefully maintains and polishes his spear. Although the Express is very safe, it seems that he is not willing to drop his guard.

t_ch: 嗯，帕姆看得出来，擦拭长枪时，他的眼神里除了警觉外，还有一丝怀念。
t_en: Hmm, Pom-Pom can tell that when he polishes his spear, there isn't just alertness in his eyes, but a glint of longing, too.

t_ch: 不要小看帕姆啊，帕姆可是和许多不同乘客打过交道的。
t_en: Don't underestimate me, Pom-Pom had dealt with all kinds of different passengers!

t_ch: 鹤运速递，啥都能寄。返航路线生成中…
t_en: Heron Express — We Mail Anything. Generating return route...

t_ch: 鹤运物流真诚感谢志愿者的协助，请收下我们的谢礼。
t_en: Heron Express wishes to sincerely thank those volunteers for their support. Please accept this as thanks.

t_ch: 前方区域风险系数较高，请及时清除高危单位，降低物流风险。
t_en: The risk factor for the area up ahead is very high. Please clear away high-risk units to reduce the impact on deliveries.

t_ch: 正在取货，并规划通往目标地点的最短路线。
t_en: Picking up delivery and plotting out the shortest route to the destination.

t_ch: 救救…
t_en: Save me...

t_ch: 前方检测到障碍物，请及时确认道路状况，保证物流通畅。
t_en: Obstacles detected dead ahead. Please verify route situation to guarantee a smooth delivery.

t_ch: 待机中的物流机巧鸟已被激活，正在前往取货地点。
t_en: The express cycrane has been taken off standby, and is now heading toward the pick-up spot.

t_ch: 物流线路已规划完成，请紧密跟随确保机巧鸟正常工作。
t_en: Logistics route planned. Please follow the cycrane closely to ensure regular operations.

t_ch: 充电中…
t_en: Charging...

t_ch: 物流机巧鸟功能检测…全功能已恢复<unbreak>76.9%</unbreak>，正在前往目标地点。
t_en: Testing functions of the express cycrane... <unbreak>76.9%</unbreak> of full function restored. Heading to the target location.

t_ch: 风险状态已经解除，正在前往目标地点。
t_en: Risk status has been reset. Heading to the target location.

t_ch: 鹤运物流真诚感谢志愿者的协助，请收下我们的谢礼。
t_en: Heron Express wishes to sincerely thank those volunteers for their support. Please accept this as thanks.

t_ch: 鹤运速递，啥都能寄。返航路线生成中…
t_en: Heron Express — We Mail Anything. Generating return route...

t_ch: 请尽快回到伴随状态。
t_en: Please return to escort mode ASAP.

t_ch: 请紧密跟随确保机巧鸟正常工作。
t_en: Please follow the cycrane closely to ensure regular operations.

t_ch: 为确保物流安全，物流机巧鸟将即刻返回停鸟架。
t_en: For security reasons, the courier cycrane will immediately return to its roost.

t_ch: 鹤运速递，啥都能寄。全新广告语，现面向全罗浮募集中。
t_en: Heron Express — We Mail Anything. The all-new slogan is being pumped out all over the Luofu.

t_ch: 中小包裹，当日可达。
t_en: Same-day delivery for small- and medium-sized parcels.

t_ch: 鹤运物流，仙舟生活最好的朋友。
t_en: Heron Express: Your Xianzhou lifestyle's best friend.

t_ch: 真诚感谢您的帮助。
t_en: I sincerely appreciate your help.

t_ch: 请收下我们的谢礼。
t_en: Please, take this as thanks.

t_ch: 鹤运速递，啥都能寄。
t_en: Heron Express — We Mail Anything.

t_ch: 返航路线生成中…
t_en: Generating return route...

t_ch: 哔——啵。
t_en: Beep — boop.

t_ch: 感谢你能担当我们的陪练，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: Thanks for being our sparring partner, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 禁区的铁卫已经布好了阵势，准备迎接你的挑战。
t_en: The Silvermane Guards have set up position in the restricted zone and are prepared to meet your challenge.

t_ch: 你需要突破我们设下的封锁线，并在终点接受邓恩队长的试炼。
t_en: You have to break through the blockade we've set up, and take on Captain Dunn's trial at the end.

t_ch: 推开眼前挡路的军械箱，继续前进吧。哔——啵。
t_en: Push away the armory box blocking your path, and continue on. Beep — boop.

t_ch: 参加本次练兵的铁卫都经过了特训。如果不小心触发了警戒状态，战斗会变得相当棘手。
t_en: The Silvermane Guards in this drill have all undergone special training. Combat against them can get pretty tricky if you trigger the alarm.

t_ch: 简单来说，他们都深谙<color=#dbc291ff>先下手为强</color>的战术思想。想避免遭到突袭的话，还是尽量<color=#dbc291ff>避开他们的视线吧</color>。
t_en: Put simply, they're all experts in the tactical art of <color=#dbc291ff>seizing the initiative</color>. If you want to avoid a surprise attack, <color=#dbc291ff>stay out of their line of sight</color> as much as possible.

t_ch: 哔——啵。封锁线后的大门被锁住了。
t_en: Beep — boop. The gate behind the blockade is locked.

t_ch: 想要继续前进，挑战邓恩队长的话，有两条路线可以选择。
t_en: There are two paths to choose from if you want to keep on going and challenge Captain Dunn.

t_ch: 选项一，蛮攻。只要击败在封锁线上巡逻的所有铁卫士兵和军官，大门就会开启。
t_en: Option 1: Brute force. Just beat up all the Silvermane soldiers and officers patrolling along the blockade, and the gate will open.

t_ch: 选项二，智取。想办法绕过巡逻铁卫的眼线，然后在侧翼区域找到控制大门的终端。
t_en: Option 2: Smarts. Find a way to bypass the patrolling guards' lines of sight, then flank them and find the terminal that controls the gate.

t_ch: 要选择哪种方式由你决定，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: The choice is yours, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: （那一定就是控制大门的终端了。）
t_en: (That must be the terminal that controls the gate.)

t_ch: （轻轻松松。果然「智取」就是我的强项。）
t_en: (Piece of cake. Looks like Smarts are my strong suit after all.)

t_ch: 哔——啵。干得漂亮，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: Beep — boop. Well done, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 大门已经打开了。请穿过封锁区，接受邓恩队长的试炼吧。
t_en: The gate's now open. Please cross the blockade and undertake Captain Dunn's trial.

t_ch: 喔，这不是{NICKNAME}教官么！让我看看时间……
t_en: Oh, if it isn't Drillmaster {NICKNAME}! Let me check the time...

t_ch: …精彩，不愧是你，用了这么短的时间就突破了我们精心布设的封锁线。
t_en: ...Marvelous. As expected, you've cut straight through our carefully-laid blockade in no time at all.

t_ch: 不过「副官」应该也跟你解释过了——想要赢得这场演练，你还得过我这关才行。
t_en: But the aide should have already explained to you that, if you want to win this drill, you'll have to get through me.

t_ch: 怎么样，{NICKNAME}？准备好再跟我较量一次了吗？
t_en: How about it, {NICKNAME}? Are you ready to go head-to-head with me?

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: …干脆！
t_en: ...Good!

t_ch: 手下败将！
t_en: I'll beat you!

t_ch: …口气不小！
t_en: ...You sure talk big!

t_ch: 来吧，{NICKNAME}！全盛状态的银鬃铁卫指挥官该有的战力，今天就让你见识见识！
t_en: Bring it, {NICKNAME}! I'll show you the strength of a Silvermane Guard commander in their prime!

t_ch: 呼…精彩…真是精彩。你的作战技巧比之前更纯熟了……
t_en: Hoo... Wonderful... Truly wonderful. Your combat skills are more proficient than before...

t_ch: 我只有两个技能而已。
t_en: I only have two abilities.

t_ch: …嗯？你在说什么呢？
t_en: ...Huh? What are you talking about?

t_ch: 那之后我又氪了不少。
t_en: After that, I gacha'ed loads more.

t_ch: …啊？你什么意思？
t_en: ...Huh? What do you mean?

t_ch: 罢了，不必详细解释。强者自有修炼秘诀，我懂！
t_en: Never mind. No need to go over the details. The strong have their own ways for getting stronger. I get it!

t_ch: 这次禁区演练就到此结束。去和「副官」聊聊，领取你应得的酬劳吧。
t_en: That's enough drills in the restricted zone for now. Go chat with the aide and claim your well-deserved reward.

t_ch: 感谢你帮助铁卫精进作战技艺——希望你能再来参加后续的演练，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: Thanks for helping the Guards hone our combat skills — We hope you can come back and train with us again sometime, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哔——啵。精彩的发挥，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: Beep — boop. Slick moves, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 请收下你应得的陪练酬劳。如果想要再次参加演练，欢迎来铁卫禁区找我登记。
t_en: Please accept this sparring reward. If you want to participate in drills again, come register with me in the restricted zone.

t_ch: 向你致敬，{NICKNAME}教官。哔——啵。
t_en: I salute you, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}. Beep — boop.

t_ch: 哔——啵。想要通过演练，就必须突破铁卫设下的封锁线，然后在终点接受邓恩队长的试炼。
t_en: Beep — boop. If you want to pass the drill, you'll have to break through the blockade that the Guards have set up, then challenge Captain Dunn's trial at the end.

t_ch: 期待你的表现，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: I look forward to your performance, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哔——啵。
t_en: Beep — boop.

t_ch: 感谢你能担当我们的陪练，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: Thanks for being our sparring partner, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 禁区的铁卫已经布好了阵势，准备迎接你的挑战。
t_en: The Silvermane Guards have set up position in the restricted zone and are prepared to meet your challenge.

t_ch: 这一次的演练目标是——击败「少校」。
t_en: The goal of this drill is to defeat the Major.

t_ch: 「少校」？
t_en: The Major?

t_ch: 该不会是…
t_en: Don't tell me that's...

t_ch: 没错。就是那边的大家伙。
t_en: That's right. It's the big guy over there.

t_ch: 笑话时间——「少校」很想减肥，他该怎么做？
t_en: Joke time: The Major wants to lose some weight. What should he do?

t_ch: 少喝机油。哈。哈。哈。
t_en: Drink less oil! ...Geddit?

t_ch: 还是直奔主题吧…
t_en: Let's cut to the chase...

t_ch: 想要挑战「少校」，就必须先<color=#dbc291ff>调度齿轮栈桥，打通道路。</color>
t_en: You'll need to <color=#dbc291ff>configure the gear bridge to make a path</color> before you challenge the Major.

t_ch: 控制栈桥的终端就在禁区里的某处。期待你的表现，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: The terminal that controls the gear bridge is somewhere in the restricted zone. I look forward to your performance, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: （这玩意……）
t_en: (This whatchamacallit...)

t_ch: （好像被锁住了呀。怎么回事？）
t_en: (Seems locked. What's going on with it?)

t_ch: 哔——啵。看来栈桥的终端被锁住了。必须使用特定的<color=#dbc291ff>终端密钥</color>才能解锁。
t_en: Beep — boop. It seems that the bridge terminal is locked. Only a special <color=#dbc291ff>terminal encryption key</color> can unlock it.

t_ch: 禁区的<color=#dbc291ff>铁卫军官</color>也许随身携带着密钥。想要操控栈桥的话，看来必须要和他们作战才行。
t_en: The <color=#dbc291ff>Silvermane officers</color> here should have the key. Looks like you'll have to fight them if you want to operate the bridge.

t_ch: （现在还不能去挑战「少校」…）
t_en: (I can't challenge the Major yet...)

t_ch: （看来还得再找到一个终端才行。）
t_en: (Looks like I'll have to find another terminal, then.)

t_ch: （成功了。）
t_en: (Got it.)

t_ch: （去会会这位「少校」吧。）
t_en: (Let's go meet this Major.)

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教官，哈！哈！哈！
t_en: Drillmaster {NICKNAME}, bwahaha!

t_ch: 久仰大名！今日一见，倍感荣幸！哈！哈！哈！
t_en: We finally meet! It's an honor to see you today. Bwahaha!

t_ch: 你就是「少校」？
t_en: Are you the Major?

t_ch: 没错，正是在下！
t_en: That's right! That's me!

t_ch: 好豪爽的机器人。
t_en: This robot gets straight to the point.

t_ch: 这是我的待客之道！
t_en: This is how I welcome people!

t_ch: 我听邓恩指挥官提及过你的实力。实不相瞒，我早就摩拳擦掌，想与你一较高下了！
t_en: I've heard Commander Dunn mention your strength. To tell the truth, I've been itching to test my might against yours for a long time now!

t_ch: ——开玩笑的，你看我这体型，拳头根本磨不到一块去！哈！哈！哈！
t_en: — Just kidding. I'm a robot, and can't itch! Bwahahaha!

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: 开始吧，{NICKNAME}教官！战斗真是让人热血澎湃！
t_en: Let's begin, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}! Combat really gets the blood flowing!

t_ch: ——虽然我压根没有血！哈！哈！哈！
t_en: — Even though I don't have any blood! Bwahaha!

t_ch: …精彩，实在精彩！果然如邓恩所说，你的实力名不虚传！
t_en: ...Amazing. Truly amazing! Dunn was right. You really do live up to your reputation!

t_ch: 看来我的能力还有很多上升空间！哈！哈！哈！
t_en: Looks like I've still got plenty of room for improvement! Bwahaha!

t_ch: 怎么上升？
t_en: How do you improve yourself?

t_ch: 简单！
t_en: Simple!

t_ch: 你要改装自己吗？
t_en: Do you modify yourself?

t_ch: 不，不，弱者才会改装！
t_en: No no, mods are for wimps!

t_ch: 从今天开始，我每天要多加五十个引体向上，再加一百个俯卧撑！哈！哈！哈！
t_en: Starting today, I'm going to do fifty more pull-ups and one hundred push-ups, every day! Bwahaha!

t_ch: 再次祝贺你完成了演练，{NICKNAME}教官！跟「副官」聊聊吧，它应该已经把报酬给你准备好了！
t_en: Congrats once more for completing the drill, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}! Chat with the aide, and it should have a prize for you!

t_ch: 请收下你应得的陪练酬劳。如果想要再次参加演练，欢迎来铁卫禁区找我登记。
t_en: Please accept this sparring reward. If you want to participate in drills again, come register with me in the restricted zone.

t_ch: 向你致敬，{NICKNAME}教官。哔——啵。
t_en: I salute you, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}. Beep — boop.

t_ch: （那个军官身上或许有能操控栈桥的密钥？）
t_en: (That officer may have the key for the bridge?)

t_ch: （直接正面起冲突不太理智，或许还有其它办法……）
t_en: (A head-on attack isn't the smartest idea here. There has to be another way...)

t_ch: 发现敌人！{NICKNAME}教官来了！
t_en: Enemy spotted! Drillmaster {NICKNAME} is here!

t_ch: 列兵，出阵！
t_en: Troops, assemble!

t_ch: （这下只能硬着头皮从正面突破了…上吧。）
t_en: (Guess I gotta bite the bullet and smash through them directly... Bring it on.)

t_ch: （那边的铁卫守着一架无人机…或许能派上用场？）
t_en: (The Guard over there is guarding some kind of drone... Maybe that could come in handy?)

t_ch: （去看看吧。）
t_en: (Go check it out.)

t_ch: （好，那个铁卫军官的注意力转移到无人机上了。）
t_en: (Okay, that Silvermane officer's distracted with that drone.)

t_ch: （这下就能从背后突袭了。去拿到他身上的密钥吧。）
t_en: (Now we can launch a surprise attack from behind. Let's go get his key.)

t_ch: 哔——啵。找到齿轮栈桥的控制终端，打通前往禁区深处的道路。
t_en: Beep — boop. Find the terminal controlling the gear bridge to open the way into the depths of the restricted zone.

t_ch: 只要击败了「少校」，演习就宣告结束。
t_en: Defeat the Major to finish the drill.

t_ch: 期待你的表现，{NICKNAME}教官。
t_en: I look forward to your performance, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教官！真没想到，我们这么快又见面了！
t_en: Drillmaster {NICKNAME}! I never thought we'd be meeting again so soon!

t_ch: 哈！哈！哈！
t_en: Bwahaha!

t_ch: 「副官」呢？
t_en: Where the aide at?

t_ch: 这种规格的禁区演练，那小子可撑不住场面啊！
t_en: That punk can't handle drills of this scale in the restricted zone!

t_ch: 这次你是话事人？
t_en: So you're the one calling the shots this time?

t_ch: 没错！这种规格的禁区演练，还是得靠我才能撑住场面啊！
t_en: That's right! You'll have to rely on me to handle drills of this scale in the restricted zone!

t_ch: 那么，就由我来为你讲解这次禁区演练的目标吧！
t_en: Here, let me explain to you the goal of this restricted zone drill!

t_ch: 面对裂界怪物的肆意入侵，我们在很多时候没有捷径可走，只能依靠「人海战术」进行抵抗。
t_en: Facing invaders from the Fragmentum, we sometimes have no other choice but to rely on strength in numbers to fight back.

t_ch: 这一次，{NICKNAME}教官，就交由你来检验银鬃铁卫部队的成色！
t_en: This time, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}, I'll leave it to you to test the mettle of these Silvermane Guards!

t_ch: 你需要在禁区内<color=#dbc291ff>战胜四十名银鬃铁卫</color>！没错，只有击败数达到这个数字，挑战才算成功！
t_en: You'll have to <color=#dbc291ff>defeat forty Silvermane Guards</color> in the restricted zone! That's right. You can only pass this challenge once you've hit that number!

t_ch: 四十？
t_en: Forty?

t_ch: 没错！哈！哈！哈！
t_en: That's right! Bwahaha!

t_ch: 我后悔了…
t_en: I'm starting to regret this...

t_ch: 晚了！哈！哈！哈！
t_en: Too late! Bwahaha!

t_ch: 还有一点！我听说杰帕德戍卫官也打算参与这次演习……
t_en: One more thing! I heard Captain Gepard plans on joining this drill, too...

t_ch: 但我们布置场地的时候他还不在现场——谁知道他什么时候会加入演习呢？
t_en: But he wasn't there when we were setting things up. Who knows when he'll join in on the drill?

t_ch: 出发吧，{NICKNAME}教官！就由你来给这群新兵蛋子好好上一课！
t_en: Get going, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}! It's up to you to teach these rookie chumps a lesson!

t_ch: （居然要战胜四十个铁卫……）
t_en: (I somehow have to defeat forty Guards...)

t_ch: （千里之行，始于足下……）
t_en: (Every journey starts with a single step...)

t_ch: 来了——他来了！杰帕德戍卫官加入了禁区演练！
t_en: He's coming — he's coming! Captain Gepard is joining in on the drill!

t_ch: 打起精神来，小伙子们！你们可不想在长官面前丢脸吧？哈！哈！哈！
t_en: Brace yourself, lads! You don't want to lose face in front of the captain, do you? Bwahaha!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}教官，在你面前的有两个选择！
t_en: Drillmaster {NICKNAME}, you now have two choices!

t_ch: 继续原来的战略，<color=#dbc291ff>击败剩下的银鬃铁卫</color>——或者<color=#dbc291ff>直接迎战杰帕德戍卫官</color>，一劳永逸！
t_en: Stick to your original strategy and <color=#dbc291ff>defeat the remaining Silvermane Guards</color> — or <color=#dbc291ff>fight against Captain Gepard</color> and wrap up the exercise once and for all!

t_ch: 你会怎么选择，{NICKNAME}教官？哈！哈！哈！
t_en: What'll it be, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}? Bwahaha!

t_ch: （三十——只差十个了！）
t_en: (Thirty — just ten more!)

t_ch: （逃开杰帕德的搜索，一鼓作气完成演练吧。）
t_en: (Escape Gepard's search and finish the drill.)

t_ch: 谢谢你愿意担当铁卫的陪练，{NICKNAME}。这些作战经验对我们来说非常宝贵。
t_en: Thanks for sparring with the Silvermane Guards, {NICKNAME}. This combat experience really is invaluable for us.

t_ch: 你这样做等于变相拯救了许多新兵的性命。我谨代替禁区全体铁卫，向你致以最崇高的敬意。
t_en: In doing this, you've actually saved the lives of many new recruits. On behalf of all the Silvermane Guards in the restricted zone, I would like to extend my highest respects to you.

t_ch: 其实我没这么伟大…
t_en: Really, I'm no big shot...

t_ch: 不知道你有没有听过这句话：论迹不论心。
t_en: Have you ever heard the line, "Judge someone on their actions, not their thoughts"?

t_ch: 能帮上忙就好。
t_en: Glad I could help.

t_ch: 再次感谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Once again, thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 那么，奖励…
t_en: So, about that reward...

t_ch: 啊，当然。报酬一定少不了。
t_en: Oh yes — remuneration is a must.

t_ch: 和「少校」聊聊吧，它应该已经把奖励准备好了。
t_en: Chat with the Major, and it should have something ready for you.

t_ch: 后会有期，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: We'll meet again, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 精彩，精彩！我无话可说，{NICKNAME}教官！你的威猛值得我世代传颂！
t_en: Amazing, amazing! I'm speechless, Drillmaster {NICKNAME}! My children would sing of your might for generations to come!

t_ch: ——虽然机器人也没法传宗接代，哈！哈！哈！
t_en: — But robots can't actually have children, bwahaha!

t_ch: 这些酬劳，你收下吧！铁卫禁区近期应该不会再组织演练了——希望你能在别处找到更多的乐子！
t_en: Take this reward! There shouldn't be any more Guard drills in the restricted zone for a while — I hope you can find fun elsewhere!
